channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Why Greenland? Remote but resource-rich island occupies a key position in a warming world
item.description: Remote, icy and mostly pristine, Greenland plays an outsized role in the daily weather experienced by billions of people and in the climate changes taking shape all over the planet.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader says his son-in-law has been kidnapped in Caracas
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Self-exiled Venezuelan opposition leader Edmundo González, who claims to have defeated President Nicolás Maduro in last year’s presidential election, said his son-in-law was kidnapped Tuesday in Venezuela's capital.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's Liberal Party in search of a new leader while dealing with Trump threats, pending election
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's Liberal Party finds itself in search of a new leader while dealing with U.S. President-elect Donald Trump's threats to impose steep tariffs on Canadian goods and with Canada's election just months away.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran's nuclear program is nearing 'the point of no return,' France's Macron says
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron is warning that Iran's nuclear program is approaching the "point of no return," calling it a top "security challenge.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden administration eases sanctions on transitional Syrian government after Assad ouster
item.description: The Biden administration has eased sanctions blocking transactions with the transitional Syrian government following the ousting of longtime dictator Bashar Assad. The move marks a gesture of outreach between the U.S. and Hay’at Tahrir al Sham (HTS), designated as a terrorist group in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US determines Sudan paramilitary committed genocide, sanctions leader
item.description: The Biden administration on Tuesday sanctioned the top general of Sudan’s Rapid Support Forces (RSF) and determined the paramilitary group has committed genocide in the country, as well as crimes against humanity and ethnic cleansing. While the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump threatens economic, not military force, to annex Canada
item.description: President-elect Trump said Tuesday he was not considering using military force to make Canada part of the United States after repeatedly musing about the idea of the country becoming the 51st state. Instead, Trump said he intended to use "economic force" against the neighbor to the north.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Trump won't rule out using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Trump on Tuesday refused to commit to not using the U.S. military to gain control of the Panama Canal, after vowing last month to take over operation of the key passageway.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Ireland joins South Africa's ICJ genocide case against Israel
item.description: Ireland filed a declaration of intervention Monday in support of South Africa’s genocide case against Israel in the International Court of Justice (ICJ).
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Elon Musk helped Trump win. Now he's looking at Europe, and many politicians are alarmed
item.description: Fresh from pouring his money and energies into helping Donald Trump win re-election, Elon Musk has trained his sights on Europe.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Controversial founder of French far right, Jean-Marie Le Pen, dies aged 96
item.description: Jean-Marie Le Pen, who founded France's far-right National Front party, died at 96 years old. Le Pen was a controversial and confrontational figure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US says Sudanese rebel force has committed genocide and imposes sanctions on the group's leaders
item.description: The Biden administration says it has determined Sudan's Rapid Support Force rebel group and proxies are committing genocide in the country's civil war.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Senegal and Chad say ousting of French troops was their sovereign decision
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Senegalese and Chadian leaders said that the ousting of French troops from their territories was their sovereign decision, rejecting remarks from French President Emmanuel Macron that suggested otherwise.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK and BABACAR DIONE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Jean-Marie Le Pen, French far-right leader known for fiery rhetoric against immigration, dies at 96
item.description: Jean-Marie Le Pen, the founder of France’s far-right National Front who was known for fiery rhetoric against immigration and multiculturalism that earned him both staunch supporters and widespread condemnation, has died.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A planned trip to Kyiv by Trump's Ukraine envoy isn't going ahead yet, top Ukrainian diplomat says
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — An upcoming trip to Kyiv by U.S. President-elect Donald Trump’s special envoy for Ukraine and Russia has been canceled but will be rescheduled, Ukrainian Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean anti-corruption agency receives new court warrant to detain impeached President Yoon
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s anti-corruption agency said it received a new court warrant on Tuesday to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol after its previous attempt was blocked by the presidential security service last week.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill 17 people in Gaza, nearly all of them women or kids
item.description: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israeli airstrikes have killed at least 17 Palestinians, nearly all of them women or children.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Afghans land in the Philippines for visa processing ahead of resettlement in US
item.description: A group of Afghan nationals arrived in the Philippines for the processing of special immigrant visas in order to resettle in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Migrants left stranded in troubled resort as Mexico disperses them far from US border
item.description: Two weeks ahead of President-elect Donald Trump’s second inauguration, Mexico continues dissolving attention-grabbing migrant caravans and dispersing migrants throughout the country to keep them far from the U.S. border, while simultaneously limiting how many accumulate in any one place.
item.dc:creator: Antonio Castillo And María Verza, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Americans sue Venezuela's Maduro for anguish caused by imprisonment, alleged torture
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — A former U.S. Marine and a Florida man who were imprisoned in Venezuela have sued President Nicolás Maduro, accusing the leftist leader of heading a vast “criminal enterprise” that has co-opted the state and uses American citizens as bargaining chips in negotiations with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Departing US ambassador to Mexico sees 'major changes' under Trump in diplomatic relationship
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — The outgoing U.S. ambassador to Mexico said Monday he anticipated “major changes” in the diplomatic relationship between the U.S. and Mexico during the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader who claimed victory over Maduro meets with Biden
item.description: Edmundo González Urrutia, the Venezuelan opposition leader who claimed to have beaten current President Nicolás Maduro in an election in July, met with President Biden on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hurdles remain as Israel and Hamas once again inch toward a ceasefire deal
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel and Hamas once again appear to be inching toward a ceasefire that could wind down the 15-month war in Gaza and bring home dozens of Israelis held hostage there. Both Israel and Hamas are under pressure from outgoing U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader who claims to have defeated Maduro meets Biden at White House
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — The Venezuelan opposition leader who claims to have defeated President Nicolás Maduro in last year’s presidential election met Monday with U.S. President Joe Biden just days ahead of Maduro's contested inauguration to a third term.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who is Pierre Poilievre? Canada's Conservative leader seeking to become next prime minister after Trudeau exit
item.description: The leader of Canada's Conservative party, Pierre Poilievre, has been leading in the polls and is favorite to become the country's next prime minister following the exit of Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis has named the first woman to head a major Vatican office, tapping an Italian nun
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis on Monday named the first woman to head a major Vatican office, appointing an Italian nun, Sister Simona Brambilla, to become prefect of the department responsible for all the Catholic Church's religious orders.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German Christmas market attack victim dies at hospital weeks later, increasing death toll to 6
item.description: A woman who was injured during the Dec. 20 attack at a German Christmas market died at a hospital on Monday, increasing the death toll to six.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian PM Justin Trudeau to announce resignation as early as Monday: report
item.description: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, facing dwindling popularity in his country, is expected to resign this week, according to the Globe and Mail. He took office in 2015.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Austrian nationalist party leader rumored to be in talks to form government
item.description: Herbert Kickl, leader of Austria's right-wing Freedom Party, met with President Alexander Van der Bellen on Sunday, further fueling rumors that he will be asked to form a government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One cigarette can decrease life expectancy by 20 minutes: Data
item.description: In 2000, data published in The BMJ estimated one cigarette would cost a person 11 minutes of their life.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: At least 30 people killed by Israeli strikes in Gaza
item.description: At least 30 people, including children, were killed in Gaza by Israeli strikes overnight and into Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, WGN 9

item.title: South Korea air crash investigators extract black box data as grieving families mourn the victims
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Grieving relatives of the victims of the South Korea plane crash gathered at the site to pay respects to their loved ones on New Year’s Day, as officials said they've extracted data from one of the retrieved black boxes to find the exact cause of the crash.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 12 in Gaza as war grinds into the new year
item.description: Israeli strikes killed at least 12 Palestinians in the Gaza Strip, mostly women and children, officials said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation
